channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Senegal Part Ways With coach Thiaw After World Cup Exit
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: It added that FSF president Abdoulaye Fall will hold a press conference on Monday to explain the "reasons behind this decision" and "to discuss the future". The post Senegal Part Ways With coach Thiaw After World Cup Exit appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham Dies at 71
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Prominent US Senator Lindsey Graham, a key ally of President Donald Trump, died on Saturday aged 71 following a “brief and sudden illness,” his office said. “On the evening of Saturday, July 11, US Senator Lindsey Graham passed away from a brief and sudden illness,” the Republican senator from South

item.title: Market Capitalisation Rises To N156.45tn As Investors Trade N220.57bn
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Nigerian Exchange Limited (NGX) extended its bullish momentum during the week as investors traded 3.65 billion shares worth N220.57bn, pushing the market capitalisation to N156.45tn after the benchmark All-Share Index (ASI) advanced by 6.35 per cent. Weekly trading statistics released by the Exc

item.title: MSSN Lagos Welcomes Safe Rescue Of Abducted Pupils, Teachers In Oyo State
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Muslim Students’ Society of Nigeria (MSSN), Lagos State Area Unit has welcomed the safe rescue of the schoolchildren and teachers who were abducted in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State, describing the development as a beacon of hope for the victims’ families and the wider com

item.title: World Cup: Semi-Final Fixtures Confirmed, Date, Time
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-final line-up has been confirmed following Argentina’s 3-1 victory over Switzerland and England’s 2-1 win against Norway. France became the first team to secure a place in the last four after defeating Morocco 2-0 on Thursday, before Spain joined them with a 2-1 victory

item.title: Bell strikes early but India building big lead in Women's Test LIVE!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Merson: Teams win leagues, X-factor wins World Cups - and Bellingham has it
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Paul Merson says Jude Bellingham has the X-factor needed to help England win the World Cup.

item.title: Nigeria Condoles With Qatar Over Death Of Father Amir
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal Government has expressed condolence to the State of Qatar following the death of His Highness Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, the Father Amir of Qatar. The condolence message was contained in a statement issued on Sunday by the spokesperson of Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Kimiebi Imom

item.title: 'Animals', Hand of God and Beckham - Argentina and England's World Cup rivalry
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: As England and Argentina prepare to go head-to-head in Wednesday's World Cup semi-final, BBC Sport looks at their footballing rivalry.

item.title: U-17 WWCQ: Busari reacts to Flamingos’ victory over Benin
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Flamingos head coach, Akeem Busari has reflected on his team’s qualification for the 2026 FIFA U-17 Women’s World Cup, DAILY POST reports. Nigeria defeated the Young Amazons 5-3 in the second leg of their final round qualifying fixture at the Stade de Kegue, Lome on Saturday. The Fl

item.title: Bauchi gets commendation over commitment to population development activities
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Bauchi State Government has been commended for its unwavering commitment to advancing population and development programmes through its partnership with the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) over the years. The commendation was given by the Country Representative of UNFPA in Nigeria, Muriel Maf

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Will Be Closed Until Further Notice, Says Iran’s Guards
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The Guards also said they would target "new enemy bases in the region" if new military action was carried out against them. The post Strait of Hormuz Will Be Closed Until Further Notice, Says Iran’s Guards appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Iran announces closure of Strait of Hormuz after strikes on US targets in Middle East
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz following a naval confrontation involving its forces and fresh strikes targeting US interests in the Middle East. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it fired a naval cruise missile at a vessel it accused of using an unauthorised r

item.title: “Nigeria Has Africa’s Highest Number of Private Jets” — Aviation Minister Keyamo
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Minister of Aviation and Aerospace Development, Festus Keyamo, has revealed that Nigeria now officially has the largest private jet fleet in Africa, with nearly 240 private jets currently operating in the country. Keyamo made the disclosure during a strategic engagement with Canadian aircraft manufa

item.title: 99 drowned in June as heatwave stifles Germany
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Germany was among the nations hit by a stifling June heatwave in western Europe, which set temperature records in several places across the country as the mercury rose as high as 41.7C. The post 99 drowned in June as heatwave stifles Germany appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Crew abandons container ship after fire in latest Hormuz attack
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The crew of a container ship abandoned the vessel after a fire broke out in the latest incident in the Strait of Hormuz, the UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) agency said early Sunday. The crew members had moved to a rescue ship, UKMTO said early in the morning, citing a ship security officer and

item.title: Hormuz Route Open Despite Iran Declaration, Maritime Group Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz’s southern route remained open to shipping on Sunday despite Iran declaring the waterway closed as tit-for-tat attacks between the US and Tehran escalated, a maritime advisory group said.

item.title: Oyo PDP Hails Rescue Of Oriire Abductees
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party, PDP, in Oyo State has congratulated the families of abductees rescued in the Oriire incident, while commending the Federal Government and security agencies for securing their release alive. In a statement signed by the state chairman, Professor Abdulrahman Akinoso and m

item.title: JUST-IN: Protesters Storm Och’Idoma Palace With Corpses As Gunmen Kill 10 In Benue
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Angry protesters on Sunday stormed the palace of the paramount ruler of the Idoma Nation, the Och’Idoma, His Royal Majesty Agabaidu Elaigwu John Odogbo, carrying the corpses of victims following fresh attacks by suspected herdsmen that reportedly claimed at least 10 lives in Otukpo Local Gover

item.title: Peller, Jarvis Announce Wedding Date
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Nigerian content creator and TikTok stars, Hamzat Habeeb Adelaja, popularly known as Peller, and Amadou Elizabeth Aminata, also known as Jarvis, have announced the date for their wedding ceremony. Sunday Telegraph had earlier reported that the duo tied the knot in a traditional ceremony in Benin Cit

item.title: England, Argentina to renew bitter rivalry in World Cup semi-final
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: England and Argentina will meet on Wednesday in a blockbuster World Cup semi-final infused with decades of bitter rivalry after they battled past Norway and Switzerland respectively in the quarter-finals. The post England, Argentina to renew bitter rivalry in World Cup semi-final appeared first on V

item.title: US Senator and Trump’s close ally, Lindsey Graham, dies
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Republican Senator Lindsey Graham, one of the closest allies of United States President Donald Trump and a leading voice on American foreign policy, has died at the age of 71 after a brief illness. A statement issued by his office said Graham died on Saturday evening following “a brief and sudden il

item.title: England, Argentina to renew bitter rivalry in World Cup semi-final
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The winner of that game will take on the victor of France's semi-final with Spain -- they meet in Texas on Tuesday -- in the final on July 19. The post England, Argentina to renew bitter rivalry in World Cup semi-final appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: The spectacle and symbolism of Khamenei’s funeral
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: How coverage of the supreme leader's funeral exposed the limits of familiar narratives about Iran.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The pair were also seen together at a restaurant in the French capital, prompting social media users to speculate about the nature of their relationship.

item.title: Former Okada rider excretes 100 wraps of methamphetamine [Video]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A former commercial motorcycle rider has been arrested by operatives of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency, NDLEA, after allegedly excreting 100 wraps of methamphetamine he ingested in Uganda. The arrest was disclosed on Sunday by the NDLEA’s Director of Media and Advocacy, Fem

item.title: Nigeria mourns Qatar’s Father Amir, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Favour Ulebor, Abuja The Federal Government has expressed condolences to the Government and people of Qatar over the death of the country’s Father Amir, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani. In a statement issued on Sunday by the spokesperson of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Kimiebi Imomotimi Ebi

item.title: Ukraine sets Russian refinery ablaze in fresh drone attack
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Russian military said Sunday morning it shot down 349 Ukrainian drones overnight, a scale of attack comparable to those seen in recent days. The post Ukraine sets Russian refinery ablaze in fresh drone attack appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Iran shuts Strait of Hormuz, launches fresh attacks on US bases
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz after its forces fired a naval cruise missile at a vessel it accused of navigating through an unauthorised route, triggering a fresh escalation in tensions with the United States and raising fears over global oil supplies. The post Iran shuts St

item.title: Ondo resident petitions EFCC, Works Ministry over Idanre-Owena road project
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A concerned resident of Idanre Local Government Area of Ondo State, Mr. Olabanji Olusesan, has petitioned the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) and the Federal Ministry of Works over the alleged non-execution of the Idanre-Owena road rehabilitation project, despite its continued classi

item.title: EKO 2026: Lagos gears up for National Intermediate Games with logo, mascot unveiling
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The unveiling took place at the Marriott Hotel in Ikeja and was attended by officials of the National Sports Commission (NSC), the Lagos State Sports Commission (LSSC), members of the Main Organising Committee (MOC) and the Local Organising Committee (LOC) The post EKO 2026: Lagos gears up for Natio

item.title: Ondo: Police arrest man for allegedly staging own kidnap
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Ondo State Police Command has arrested a 33-year-old man, Sikiru Olawale, for allegedly staging his own kidnapping in a bid to extort money from his family. The command also arrested one of his alleged accomplices, Agbelusi Ayomide, 23, during an investigation into the incident. Speaking on the

item.title: US Senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The influential politician had just returned from Kyiv, where he met Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday.

item.title: World Cup: Bellingham becomes 7th player in history to score twice in consecutive knockout matches
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: England midfielder, Jude Bellingham, on Saturday, made history after scoring a brace in his side’s World Cup quarter-final 2-1 win against Norway. Two goals from Bellingham ensured England eliminated Norway from the competition and qualified for the semi-final stage. The Three Lions will

item.title: First Lady Urges Davido, Burna Boy, Asake To Help Poor Nigerians
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Nigeria’s First Lady, Oluremi Tinubu, has called on leading Afrobeats stars Davido, Burna Boy and Asake to complement government efforts by supporting vulnerable Nigerians through charitable initiatives. Mrs Tinubu made the appeal on Saturday during the launch of the National Community Food Ba

item.title: Africa richest continent, can feed entire world if governed by good leaders – Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, presidential candidate for the 2027 general elections, Peter Obi, says with its rich mineral resources, Africa can feed the entire world if governed by good leaders. Obi said this in a statement posted on his verified X handle on Sunday. His remark stems fr

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Isaac Fayose, younger brother to former Ekiti State Governor, Ayodele Fayose, has disagreed with the comments made by Nigeria’s First Lady, Mrs Remi Tinubu, calling on Nigerian artistes to help poor Nigerians. In a video posted on X, Fayose criticised the First Lady for asking state governors

item.title: Iran, Oman end Hormuz talks without breakthrough
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Repeated exchanges of fire between Iran and the U.S. over the past week have undermined an interim agreement reached last month to end the conflict and reopen the vital waterway.

item.description: ‎A faith-based civil liberties organisation, the Muslim Rights Concern (MURIC) has lauded President Bola Ahmed Tinubu, Governor Seyi Makinde of Oyo State and the Nigerian security agencies over the successful rescue of 46 kidnap victims at Oriire, near Ogbomosho, Oyo State. ‎ ‎Reacting to the news o

item.title: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies: Reactions
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: After Sheikh Hamad's death, global leaders offer condolences, praise his legacy and Qatar’s growth.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Parent-Teacher Association (PTA) of the Federal Government College (FGC) Staff School, Sokoto, has called for an upward review of teachers’ salaries and improved welfare packages to enhance the quality of education in the school. The resolution was reached on Saturday during the associatio

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The United States (US) President, Donald Trump’s ally and prominent American Senator, Lindsey Graham passed away on Saturday, July 11, at the she of 71. Sunday Telegraph gathered that Lindsey Graham died following a “Brief and sudden illness.” According to a statement by the Republican senator

item.title: Nigerian TikTok stars Jarvis, Peller reveal wedding date
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigerian TikTok stars Jarvis and Peller have officially announced that they will tie the knot on August 1, 2026, ending months of speculation about their relationship. The post Nigerian TikTok stars Jarvis, Peller reveal wedding date appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Singer Toluwanisings, crew members survive ghastly car accident
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian gospel singer, Toluwanisings and his crew members have been involved in a ghastly car accident. The accident which occurred involved the band’s bus and a full loaded trailer occurred on Saturday morning when the band was returning from a gospel ministration. While

item.title: Ranger FC Denies Tribalism Allegations By Footballer Moses Simon
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: Rangers Football Club has strongly denied the claim that it did not pick Footballer Moses Simon because he is from the North and not from the Eastern part of Nigeria... The club says it is not tribal and picks players based on talent alone. They pointed to their long history of signing players from

item.description: Mr Patrick my guy captined this hilarious photo with ''With the way I feel about this right now, make every body meet me for station. Both Caramel oo, Anita Joseph oo, AI oo.Make all of una Dey come zone 6 now!''

item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project, SERAP, said it has filed a lawsuit against the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, seeking a court order compelling the electoral body to investigate allegations that governors elected on the platform of the All Progressives Congress

item.title: School graduations: Rising costs put parents under pressure
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “By God’s grace, we are happy they are moving to senior secondary school, but paying for the graduation is one thing and buying what they will wear is another."

item.title: 2027 Presidential Election: The Opposition Must Unite Or Lose
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: One principle has consistently guided my public interventions on political issues: I do not allow personal emotions or political preferences to distort my analysis of matters affecting the public interest. Like every human being, I have my political biases. However, I have built my reputation by loo

item.title: England, Argentina set up World Cup showdown after quarter-final wins
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: England and Argentina set up a blockbuster World Cup semi-final showdown on Saturday after battling past Norway and Switzerland to book their passage to the last four. France 24's Selina Sykes tells us more.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has paid an emotional tribute to Senator Lindsey Graham following the veteran lawmaker’s sudden death, describing him as one of the finest people and public servants he had ever known. In a message shared on his Truth Social platform, Trump praised the South Carolin

The First Lady made this call at the launch of the National Community F

item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: She captioned the photos''As seen on so so medium by queenmothersupdatesUna don turn this outfit to Aso Ebi ........ na celebrant and venue remainThe Memory Of You will be showing on the 12th of July by 3pm on YouTube. Please subscribe, like, and share Link in my bio''smart one....

item.title: Lindsey Graham, pivotal US Republican senator, dies suddenly at 71
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US Senator Lindsey Graham, a key Republican who went from a vocal critic of Donald Trump to one of his most loyal allies on Capitol Hill after Trump became president, has died at age 71. Our correspondent Fraser Jackson tells us more.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: VARiables, politics, and Africa
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: "Stepping on an opponent's foot is a foul, whereas a defender who touches the ball first and then makes normal football contact has not committed a foul," Pierluigi Collina has said. The post FIFA World Cup: VARiables, politics, and Africa appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Police operatives under Yobe State Command have arrested two suspected members of a kidnapping syndicate and recovered 26 stolen goats during an operation in Gujba Local Government Area. According to a press statement issued on Sunday by the Police Public Relations Officer, SP Dungus Abdulkarim, the

item.title: Iran attacks five Gulf nations, shuts Hormuz after US bombing: All to know
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Tehran hits back at several GCC countries, following the most intense US bombing since an MoU was signed last month.

item.title: US strikes Iran, Tehran hits Gulf states, says Strait of Hormuz closed
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US and Iranian forces exchanged heavy missile and drone assaults with Tehran targeting US facilities in states across the Gulf on Sunday and saying it had again closed the vital Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Rising costs of school graduations put parents under pressure
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Mrs Mojisola Fatai, whose son and daughter are both graduating from JSS 3, said she was grateful to witness the milestone, but the financial burden was enormous. The post Rising costs of school graduations put parents under pressure appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.description: Navigating flooded roads is often an unavoidable nightmare for many motorists, especially during the peak of the rainy season. The post Car suspension care: How to inspect shock absorber bushings after flood driving navigations appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Russian strikes kill eight in Ukraine, officials say
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Russia struck Ukraine with missiles, drones and guided bombs on Saturday, killing eight people and wounding dozens of others, officials said.

item.title: INEC Extends Deadline For Presidential, NASS Candidates’ Submission
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has extended the deadline for political parties to submit the names and personal particulars of their candidates for the 2027 presidential and National Assembly elections by three days. The Commission, in an announcement made available to newsmen

item.title: Firefighters gain upper hand on deadly Spain wildfire
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Firefighters battling a wildfire in southern Spain that killed 12 people started to contain the blaze on Saturday as the hardest-hit village remained deserted, with charred vegetation and blackened homes a grim reminder of the inferno that sent terrified people fleeing from their homes.

item.title: TAPE 2026: When Africa lifted one voice in praise
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: On an unforgettable night, The Rock Cathedral in Lekki, Lagos, became the epicenter of fervent worship as thousands of believers gathered for the 2026 edition of The African Praise Experience (TAPE). The post TAPE 2026: When Africa lifted one voice in praise appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Anglican Church rejects Islamisation of Nigeria, Sharia integration
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Anglican Church has stoutly rejected any move to turn Nigeria into an Islamic country, as well as integrate the Sharia laws and monetary policy in the country’s financial system. The 3-day 3rd Session of the 12th Synod 2026 of Anglican Diocese of Calabar climaxed yesterday at Holy Trinity

item.description: Switzerland head coach Murat Yakin has criticised the refereeing decisions that he believes cost his side in their defeat to Argentina at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. item.title: Experts Urge Broader Economic Reforms Beyond Minimum Wage Increases
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Two public policy experts have urged the federal government to adopt broader economic reforms beyond periodic increases in the national minimum wage, warning that salary reviews alone cannot improve the living conditions of most Nigerians. Badm

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Russia is facing a fuel shortage as repeated Ukrainian strikes on its oil refining infrastructure have disrupted production.

item.description: NEWMAP has begun desilting drainage channels in Uyo after severe flooding, while officials dismiss reports claiming two people died. The post NEWMAP begins desilting gutters after Uyo flood disaster appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

The post Nigeria tourism needs destination marketing, better infra

item.title: Ex-Minister Celebrates Birthday With Students Of School For The Deaf
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Former Minister of State for Transportation, Prince Ademola Adegoroye, has donated educational materials and grinding machines to students of the School for the Deaf as part of activities marking his birthday. In addition to the donations, Adegoroye, accompanied by friends, family members, and polit

item.title: Hottest Footballer In The World Maduka Okoye Shares New Selfie...
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: Nigerian footballer Maduka Okoye shared a hot new selfie on Saturday, July 11, 2026, right in the middle of his viral moment as the “hottest footballer in the world.” Social media has been buzzing nonstop, with ladies flooding timelines with “Awwws” and “Wooows”, while the men keep asking what exact

item.title: Trump Ally, Longtime US Senator Lindsey Graham Dies At 71
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Graham was first elected to the US House of Representatives in 1994, before being elected to the Senate in 2002. The post Trump Ally, Longtime US Senator Lindsey Graham Dies At 71 appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: AEC Seeks Interconnected African Electricity Market Amid Persistent Grid Failures
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The African Energy Chamber (AEC) has called for concerted efforts to develop interconnected electricity market for Africa to enable the continent achieve its industrial potentials. The Chamber sees constant grid systems struggle to keep pace with rapidly expanding supply pipelines and rising demand

item.title: Oyo rescue shows Tinubu’s security strategy yielding results, says Kekemeke
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Commends troops, urges citizens’ support for security agencies By John Alechenu ABUJA — All Progressives Congress (APC) chieftain, Duerimini Kekemeke, has said the successful rescue of abducted pupils and teachers in Oyo State demonstrates that President Bola Tinubu’s security strategy is producing

item.title: Top 20 richest countries by average wealth per adult in 2026
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: In this article, Nairametrics examines the world's top 20 countries by average wealth per adult, based on data from the UBS Global Wealth Report 2026. The post Top 20 richest countries by average wealth per adult in 2026 appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.description: Oba Rashidi Adewolu Ladoja, the Olubadan of Ibadan, has paid a condolence visit to Bode Market in order to condole read more Olubadan pays condolence visit to Bode market gutted by fire

item.title: TikTokers Jarvis, Peller announce wedding date
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Jarvis and Peller, popular Nigerian TikTok content creators, have announced that they will tie the knot on Aug. 1.

item.description: …Extols Otti’s investments in human human capital development Maruf Tunji Àlausa, minister of Education, officially commissioned the Ogbo Central Smart read more Education minister commissions smart school in Abia

item.title: 2027 Elections: INEC Extends Candidate Submission Deadline
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Explaining the decision, INEC said the extension followed appeals from political parties that had encountered difficulties meeting the deadline. The post 2027 Elections: INEC Extends Candidate Submission Deadline appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham dies at 71
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Prominent United States Senator Lindsey Graham, a key ally of President Donald Trump, has died at the age of 71 following a “brief and sudden illness,” his office has announced. The Republican senator from South Carolina’s office said in a statement on his official X account that Graham died on the

item.title: Actor Onyi Alex Expresses Gratitude To God And Fellow Actors After Sucessful Surgery
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: Nollywood actor Onyi Alex is thanking God after undergoing surgery.(The comment section of the post she did whisper that she had a goiter OP)In a video shared from her hospital bed, she said she is grateful for God's protection throughout the experience. According to her, the scar from the surgery i

item.title: Africa Moves To Close AI Gap As ATU, UN Seal Digital Infrastructure Pact
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The African Telecommunications Union (ATU) has signed a strategic partnership with the United Nations Office for Digital and Emerging Technologies (UN-ODET) to strengthen artificial intelligence (AI) capacity and accelerate the development of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) across Africa, as the

item.title: Watch World Cup: England beats Norway to set up semifinal against Argentina
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Latest match highlights, reaction and previews from the FIFA World Cup 2026 in North America.

item.title: US, Iran Trade Wave of Attacks as Tehran Says Hormuz Closed
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: US forces struck Iran for a third time in a week, prompting retaliatory attacks from Tehran as it declared the Strait of Hormuz would be closed “until further notice.”
